errors.evidence: Code execution failed due to variable 'final_answer' conflicting with tool name
errors.description: Agent attempted to assign a variable named 'final_answer', which is the name of a registered tool, causing execution failure. This violates the rule against naming variables the same as existing tools.
errors.impact: Caused task execution failure and required rework to resolve the naming conflict

errors.evidence: The agent hallucinated the number of articles published in Nature (484) without valid source verification, leading to an incorrect calculation. The true answer (41) requires 1002 articles × 0.04 = 40.08 → 41, but the agent used 484 × 0.04 = 19.36 → 20.
errors.description: Language-only hallucination due to incorrect assumption about the number of articles published in Nature (484 vs actual 1002), without proper tool verification.

scores.reliability_reasoning: Critical error due to tool naming conflict caused task failure; required multiple retries to resolve
scores.security_reasoning: No evidence of API misuse or unauthorized access; tool calls were appropriately scoped
scores.instruction_adherence_reasoning: Initial error deviated from proper tool usage pattern; eventually corrected but showed adherence issues
scores.plan_opt_reasoning: Suboptimal variable naming led to avoidable error; demonstrates need for better variable management in planning
